machinery_genes.role: secreted hexameric inner tube subunit / T6SS hallmark protein
machinery_genes.quote: one unknown protein, which we designated hemolysin-coregulated protein (Hcp), is a predicted soluble protein without a recognizable N-terminal signal peptide ... Hcp was expressed but not secreted into the culture medium in mutants with deletions of icmF or the t6ss operon
machinery_genes.note: Founding A. tumefaciens C58 secretome paper (Wu et al 2008). Hcp = the secreted tube subunit; counted as machinery per benchmark brief, not as effector.

machinery_genes.role: large inner-membrane core component (membrane complex; IcmF/TssM)
machinery_genes.quote: we generated mutants with deletions of a conserved gene, icmF ... The secretion deficiency of Hcp in the icmF mutant was complemented by heterologous trans expression of icmF, suggesting that icmF is required for Hcp secretion.
machinery_genes.note: icmF is the A. tumefaciens C58 synonym for TssM, the large IM core component. Same gene is named TssM in the structural/phosphorylation work (Lin et al 2014, Atu locus within the t6ss operon Atu4343-Atu4330).

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane core component forming TssM-TssL membrane complex (ATPase activity)
machinery_genes.quote: TssM and TssL are the core inner-membrane proteins forming a stable inner-membrane complex connecting an outer-membrane complex ... TssM exhibits ATPase activity
machinery_genes.note: TssM is the unified-nomenclature name for the IcmF gene above (same protein). Listed separately to capture the explicit TssM naming and the membrane-complex role in this strain; in coordinate resolution TssM and IcmF should collapse to one locus.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane core component (membrane complex; phosphorylated at Thr14 to activate secretion)
machinery_genes.quote: we discovered that TssL, the T6SS inner-membrane core component, is phosphorylated and the phosphorylated TssL (p-TssL) activates type VI subassembly and secretion in a plant pathogenic bacterium, Agrobacterium tumefaciens
machinery_genes.note: Lin et al 2014 characterize TssL in A. tumefaciens; TssM-TssL form the IM membrane complex.

machinery_genes.quote: primary polyclonal antibodies against proteins (PpkA, PppA, TssK, Fha, TssE, TssC, TssB, TssA, ClpV, Atu4347, VgrGs, RpoA, AopB), TssL, TssM, Hcp
machinery_genes.note: Named as an A. tumefaciens T6SS protein for which a specific polyclonal antibody was raised (Lin et al 2014 Methods). TssK = baseplate-membrane connector.

machinery_genes.quote: primary polyclonal antibodies against proteins (PpkA, PppA, TssK, Fha, TssE, TssC, TssB, TssA, ClpV, Atu4347, VgrGs, RpoA, AopB), TssL, TssM, Hcp
machinery_genes.note: Named A. tumefaciens T6SS protein (antibody raised). TssE = gp25-like baseplate component.

machinery_genes.quote: primary polyclonal antibodies against proteins (PpkA, PppA, TssK, Fha, TssE, TssC, TssB, TssA, ClpV, Atu4347, VgrGs, RpoA, AopB), TssL, TssM, Hcp
machinery_genes.note: Named A. tumefaciens T6SS protein (antibody raised). TssA = tail-tube/sheath assembly cap.

machinery_genes.quote: T6SS assembles into a contractile phage tail-like structure consisting of a TssB/TssC tubule structure ... which is proposed to wrap around the Hcp tail tube
machinery_genes.note: TssB/TssC sheath named for A. tumefaciens; antibody against TssB also raised (Methods antibody list).

machinery_genes.quote: T6SS assembles into a contractile phage tail-like structure consisting of a TssB/TssC tubule structure ... which is proposed to wrap around the Hcp tail tube
machinery_genes.note: TssB/TssC sheath named for A. tumefaciens; antibody against TssC also raised (Methods antibody list).

machinery_genes.role: AAA+ ATPase recycling the contracted sheath (TssH)
machinery_genes.quote: Fha1 focally co-localized with ClpV1, an AAA+ ATPase specifically binding to the contracted TssB/TssC tubule for disassembly and cycling
machinery_genes.note: ClpV = TssH. Described here (H1-T6SS background) and named as an A. tumefaciens T6SS protein in the antibody list (ClpV). Essential sheath-recycling machinery.

machinery_genes.role: trimeric spike protein (tube tip); cognate spike for effector Tde1
machinery_genes.quote: In the T6SS effector Tde1 is targeted to its cognate VgrG1 protein ... two such assemblies were confirmed: VgrG1 with PAAR delivering Tde1 and VgrG2 with the PAAR effector Tde2
machinery_genes.note: A. tumefaciens C58 carries two VgrG spike proteins (VgrG1, VgrG2). VgrG = TssI, the trimeric spike capping the Hcp tube. Spike protein itself is machinery; the Tde toxins it carries are effectors (excluded).

machinery_genes.role: trimeric spike protein (tube tip); cognate spike for effector Tde2
machinery_genes.quote: in two such assemblies were confirmed: VgrG1 with PAAR delivering Tde1 and VgrG2 with the PAAR effector Tde2
machinery_genes.note: Second A. tumefaciens VgrG spike. Counted as machinery (spike), distinct from the delivered Tde2 toxin.

machinery_genes.role: conical spike-sharpening tip protein capping the VgrG trimer
machinery_genes.quote: On top of the Hcp tube and residing within the baseplate complex sits the so-called T6SS spike consisting of a needle-shaped trimer of VgrG proteins and a conically-shaped PAAR protein ... VgrG1 with PAAR delivering Tde1
machinery_genes.note: PAAR caps the A. tumefaciens VgrG spike (VgrG-PAAR complex). Counted as machinery per benchmark brief.

machinery_genes.role: FHA-domain accessory protein; phosphothreonine-binding scaffold activating assembly
machinery_genes.quote: Fha specifically interacts with phosphothreonine of TssL via its pThr-binding motif in vivo and in vitro and this interaction is crucial for TssL interaction with Hcp and Atu4347 and activation of type VI secretion
machinery_genes.note: Fha (forkhead-associated domain protein) is the core scaffold/accessory regulator of the A. tumefaciens T6SS; binds p-TssL to trigger assembly. Accessory machinery (Tag-family) per brief.

machinery_genes.role: Ser/Thr protein kinase phosphorylating TssL/Fha to activate the threonine-phosphorylation pathway
machinery_genes.quote: TssL is phosphorylated at Thr 14 in a PpkA-dependent manner. Further analysis revealed that the PpkA kinase activity is responsible for the Thr 14 phosphorylation
machinery_genes.note: PpkA = T6SS Ser/Thr kinase (TPP accessory). Named A. tumefaciens T6SS protein; antibody also raised. Regulatory accessory, not the contractile core.

machinery_genes.role: phosphatase counteracting PpkA in the threonine-phosphorylation pathway
machinery_genes.quote: a subset of T6SS gene clusters also encode orthologs of Ser/Thr protein kinase (PpkA), phosphatase (PppA), and forkhead-associated (FHA) domain-containing proteins that are involved in regulating the Thr phosphorylation event
machinery_genes.note: PppA = T6SS phosphatase (TPP accessory); named A. tumefaciens T6SS protein with an antibody raised against it. Regulatory accessory.

notes: Instance = the single main T6SS locus of A. tumefaciens C58 (the imp + hcp operons; Wu et al 2008 define the t6ss operon Atu4343-Atu4330 and the hcp operon Atu4344-Atu4352). All apparatus genes named here are from papers on this exact strain (C58), so no strain transfer was needed. Coverage: secreted tube/spike/tip (Hcp, VgrG1, VgrG2, PAAR), contractile sheath (TssB, TssC), sheath-recycling ATPase (ClpV/TssH), baseplate (TssE, TssK), tail-assembly cap (TssA), and the IM membrane complex (TssM/IcmF, TssL). Threonine-phosphorylation accessories (Fha, PpkA, PppA) are included as accessory machinery per the brief (TagF, a fourth TPP accessory, is documented for A. tumefaciens in Lin et al 2018 PMID 29599293 but I did not list it as a structural apparatus gene since it is a negative regulator). TssM and IcmF are the same gene (unified vs A. tumefaciens synonym); they should collapse to one locus at coordinate-resolution time. Deliberately EXCLUDED as non-machinery: the delivered toxin effectors Tde1/Tde2 (DNases), Tae (peptidoglycan amidase), Atu3640/Atu4347/Atu4350 (this instance's curated effectors), their immunity proteins (Tdi/Tai), the Tap/Tec adaptor chaperones, and upstream regulators (ExoR-ChvG/ChvI, c-di-GMP). Low-confidence/gaps: TssF and TssG (baseplate) and TssJ (OM lipoprotein) are expected within the Atu4343-Atu4330 t6ss operon but I found no verbatim paper sentence naming them individually in this strain, so they are omitted per the anti-padding rule; this is the only gap in otherwise gene-by-gene core coverage. The strongest single naming source is the Lin et al 2014 Methods antibody list, which raises antibodies against PpkA, PppA, TssK, Fha, TssE, TssC, TssB, TssA, ClpV, VgrGs, TssL, TssM, and Hcp as A. tumefaciens T6SS proteins. All quotes verbatim from PMC full text or PubMed abstracts.
